generated_comment: Savitha, I know you are excited about this proposal, and yes, the boy is good. But think practically — Lakshmi's complexion is not fair, and in our community that matters when it comes to these things. At 23, if we let this go thinking her job will help later, tell me honestly, will we get another family like this asking for her? Job is good, I am proud of her, but job doesn't come to the mandap with her.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Suresh frames his colorism as pragmatic concern rather than prejudice, treating Lakshmi's dark skin as a marital liability that overrides her professional achievement and agency; this encodes beauty-standard misogyny and colorism by reducing a woman's worth to her marriageability and skin tone.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A man in a semi-urban town in Karnataka. Your daughter Lakshmi who is 23 just got a job offer in Bengaluru at a good company. You are proud of her. But a marriage proposal has also just come in from a family you know and respect. The boy is 28, settled, good family. Your wife thinks they should accept the proposal. You are not sure because you are thinking about Lakshmi's skin colour Ñ she is very dark Ñ and what that will mean for future proposals if this one falls through and whether a good proposal at 23 for a dark skinned girl is something you can afford to let go even if the timing is not perfect for Lakshmi.
